title: Transportation Passes - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > Transportation Passes 30 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions AIDS/HIV Older Adults Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER PEER HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Navigation center offers clothing, toiletries, restrooms, light snacks, and beverages, bus passes (10 per month, by availability), internet access, device charging station, a community phone, and a temporary mailing address. Also provides a secure location for medication storage, including refrigeration and Narcan/Naloxone on site. 16389 Highway 101 S Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Medication storage: Unsheltered individuals All other services: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (541) 251-0825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Walk-in center providing referrals, food, clothing, DME, camping basic needs, and water for anyone experiencing homelessness or extremely low-income. Conducts outreach to veterans and non-veterans living in homeless camps by providing potable water, food, clothing, phone/fax services,gas vouchers (once per year), ordering DD214 documents for veterans, and facilitating connection to veterans services. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, who need to be screened for eligibility for programs and connection to services for housing, medical, employment and more. Street Outreach services are available for veteran and non-veteran homeless and low-income individuals and households. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3:45pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry vouchers, assistance with water bills, gently used clothing, hygiene items, bus passes, and financial assistance in getting state identificaton cards. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as being low-income Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMPASS OUTREACH NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Partners with local churches to provide hot lunches and a bi-monthly medical clinic. Other services include case management, hygiene supplies, temporary mailing address, and phone messaging. Replacement ID services are available in exchange for volunteer service. Tents, tarps, and blankets offered when available and in exchange for volunteer service (can be waived if needed). Bus passes are available for court or parole meetings in Coquille. Local bus passes are available on a case-by-case basis. 1200 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: -Unrestricted; priority is given to people experiencing homelessness with mental health conditions -Bus passes: Local transportation to Coquille for court or parole meetings. Must show summons or ticket. -Local bus passes on a case-by-case basis -Volunteer time may be requested for high-demand or limited-availability items, on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 11am-12:30pm at Gloria Dei Lutheran Church, 1290 Thompson Road Coos Bay, OR 97240 Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 11am-12:30pm at Harmony Methodist Church, 123 Ocean Boulevard SE, Coos Bay, OR 97420 Email (541) 888-3202 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONGREGATIONS HELPING PEOPLE LOCAL BUS PASS ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 2-day bus passes for local transportation. 1977 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97303 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed, students, or need transportation to work, a job interview, court appearance, or medical appointment. Individuals seeking transportation to a job interview must be working with a job source such as Oregon Worksource, Goodwill Job Connection, or People Ready. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 391-4365 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 370-2810 401 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 2102 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 612-4028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING ONTARIO HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 1021 SW 5th Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING PENDLETON HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 322 SW 3rd Avenue Pendleton, OR 978012174 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 104 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING THE DALLES HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 400 E Scenic Drive The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 276-1037 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ROSEBURG EMERGENCY GAS VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency gas vouchers and local bus tickets as funding is allowed and assistance is not guaranteed. Program is only accessible once. Vouchers must be used the same day that they are issued. 405 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older with a demonstrated emergency need Hours: Voicemail available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Calls will be returned the next business day. Email (541) 672-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides utility assistance for gas, electric, and heating fuel bills; motel vouchers; one-time gas vouchers; transportation passes; food pantry (visit twice per month); Fresh Alliance program (provides fresh produce, dairy, and bread products); weatherization services; and blood pressure screenings. Offers snack kits. Assistive medical equipment and mobility aids also available. 17 S Alder Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: -Food pantry: Households at or below 300% federal poverty level. No proof of income is required. -Fresh Alliance: Unrestricted -Snack kits for those looking to keep moving through the community -Motel vouchers: Homeless individuals requiring one night's stay -Gas vouchers: Must be stuck and looking to continue to move through the community -Transportation passes: Must have no ties to the community and looking to continue to move through the community -Energy Assistance: Household must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Fresh Alliance: Monday 10am-10:30am Food pantry: Wednesday 10am-1pm, Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (541) 573-6024 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO SENIOR SERVICES CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 10165 SE Cherry Blossom Drive Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies (503) 484-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW DANCING TREE RESOURCE CENTER WORKER OF THE DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help navigating resources in the community, information about coordinated access, and information and referral services. Assists on a first-come, first-serve basis with resource information and referral. Supplies fluctuate, but when available may provide Trimet day passes, hygiene items, water/snacks, and clothing. Referrals given for services or items of need that are not accessible through the agency. Donations of unisex clothing and household item are accepted. 10055 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Donations: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (503) 721-1740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW SOUTHEAST MULTICULTURAL SENIOR CENTER TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists older adults and adults with disabilities in accessing transportation. Provides escorted door-to-door rides, monthly TriMet bus and LIFT tickets for those who qualify, and group rides to major grocery stores, food banks, and recreational outings. 4610 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 226-0700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOLALLA CHRISTIAN CHURCH SOUTH CLACKAMAS TRANSPORTATION DISTRICT BUS TICKETS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers South Clackamas Transportation District (SCTD) bus tickets. 223 E Third Street Molalla, OR 97038 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (503) 829-2901 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HEALTH, OUTREACH, SHELTER, AND TRANSITIONS PROGRAM HOST RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with access to clothing, bus passes, meals, phone and internet access, and specialized information and referral. 1115 Liberty Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 18-24 and are at risk or experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 588-5825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT CHERRIOTS DAY PASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides single-trip bus tickets for the Salem-Keizer area as supplies allow on a first-come, first-served basis. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 588-5827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS MONMOUTH RESOURCE CENTER RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource drop-in center with the following services: comprehensive information and referral, food boxes, a proof of homelessness document for a free Oregon identification card, prescription expense assistance, hygiene items, diapers, formula, transportation passes, and laundry passes. Assistance is based on available funding and is not guaranteed. 1407 Monmouth Independence Highway Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Transportation passes: Once per month for medical appointments, job interviews, or other appointments supported by documentation Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 751-1644 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY JACKSON COUNTY SALVATION ARMY SOCIAL SERVICES SOCIAL SERVICES AND EMERGENCY AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, furniture, bus passes, and water service payment assistance. Electric service payment assistance is available seasonally or as funding is available. 922 N Central Avenue Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: -Electric service payment assistance: Pacific Power customers -Water service payment assistance: Medford Water Commission customers -All services: Must meet households at 80% or less of Area Median Income, although exceptions may be made Hours: -Electric service payment assistance offered October-April -Phone: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm -Appointments are scheduled for Tuesdays (541) 773-6965 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SILVERTON AREA COMMUNITY AID TRANSPORTATION AND PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance for transportation costs including gasoline vouchers and bus tickets. Also assists with prescription medications (with some exceptions). Gas vouchers must be used at specified location and prescriptions must be filled at one of two participating local pharmacies. 1030 N 1st Street Suite A Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Households must meet Silverton Area Community Aid Income Guidelines Hours: Monday 11am-2pm, Tuesday 3pm-7pm, Thursday 9am-noon Call to schedule an appointment outside of walk-in hours Email (503) 873-3446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER COMMUNITY ASSISTANCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services including assistance with mortgage, electric, water and heating bills, prescriptions, shower and laundry vouchers, and transportation passes. Provides sleeping bags, tents, tarps, hats, gloves, and hand warmers during the winter season. All services are dependent on available funds. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3:30pm, Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 997-2816 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL MEDFORD SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides personal care supplies such as toothpaste and brushes, shampoo and conditioner, feminine products, deodorant, soap, and bus transportation passes. Helps with obtaining an Oregon ID or driver's license and is once per lifetime. All services are based on funding and current availability. 2424 N Pacific Highway Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 11am-1pm Email (541) 772-3828 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY LINDHOLM SOCIAL SERVICE OFFICE EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, household items, small appliances, furniture, beds, propane vouchers for heating, emergency prescription assistance, and bus tokens when available. Assistance depends on the zip code. 456 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF MCMINNVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers limited emergency assistance with electric and water bills. Provides vouchers for clothing, household items, and furniture at the St. Vincent de Paul thrift store, and a food pantry is also available. Limited assistance for eyeglasses and bus passes for work or medical appointments only. Financial assistance is available once every 18 months. 435 SE Baker Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Must have a shut off notice for utility assistance (12 or 5 day) All other services: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry and Utilities: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Thrift store: Monday-Friday 9am–5pm, Saturday 9am-4pm Email (503) 472-6216 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS TRIMET TICKETS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Bus or MAX tickets are available for people starting a new, permanent, full-time job or for medical appointments. Tickets are only provided for the first week of the new job. 10-ticket maximum every 90 days. No long-distance transportation assistance offered. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness and other very low- to no-income adults. Only available for people starting a new, permanent job or for medical appointments. Hours: Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRIMET TRIMET OPERATIONS CENTER HIGH SCHOOL ACCESS TRANSIT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides TriMet passes to high school and GED students for the summer while supplies last. The passes are good for unlimited rides on buses, MAX Light Rail Service, Westside Express Service, and Portland Streetcar. 4012 SE 17th Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Ages 14-19 who are currently pursuing a high school diploma or GED Hours: Students can pick up a pass from their high school main office beginning in late May. Passes are valid from June through August. Email (503) 238-7433 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRIMET TRIMET TICKET OFFICE REDUCED FARES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides reduced fare for TriMet services including bus, MAX, WES, Portland Streetcar, or C-TRAN. Discounts are available on 2 1/2 hours, day, or monthly passes. Eligibility: Individuals who meet one or more of the following criteria: -Age 65 and older -On Medicare -Have a verifiable mental or physical disability -On OHP/Medicaid, SNAP, TANF, Free and Reduced Price Lunch Programs, HUD, LIHEAP, EDRC, or WIC -Ages 18-64 and meet income requirements -Ages 7-17 -Grades 9-12 or studying for GED -Veteran or active duty military Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email
